messages.message: I believe we could benefit from a strategic alliance. With our mutual interests in controlling key supply centers in the Mediterranean, coordinating our efforts could lead to greater success. Let's discuss potential cooperation.
Hi Italy! The standard IA is always in the way, so I'm happy to go another route if you're interested.

I am interested in exploring potential cooperation in the Adriatic region. Our positions are adjacent, and aligning our strategies could help us secure key supply centers more effectively. Let's talk about possible moves.
Hi Turkey! I'll try to give you a quick idea of my early moves, then I'd be happy to talk in the fall if that sounds good to you.

Hi Germany! I've got to say that, in my limited experience as either Austria or Germany, I love the triple alliance between us, even if it doesn't get much done until later. It's so hard to stab each other and the alliance can work for so long.
Hi Russia! The IA alliance can be strong if played correctly, so I'd be happy to work with you. I'm looking for a DMZ in Galicia, if you're interested.

I see that we both have interests in the Adriatic and the Balkans. Perhaps we could coordinate our movements to prevent other powers from gaining too much influence in the region. Let's discuss a possible alliance.

Hey France,
I think England and France are natural allies. We work best together by not fighting and focusing our forces on our natural enemies. DMZ in English channel and no fleet builds in Brest would make me very happy.

Hey Austria!
That sounds like a plan. I think we could help one another out pretty effectively in this early game, if you're interested. I can of course open to the Balkan Gambit, but if you'd like, we can also blitz Russia and get things rolling early.

Hi Austria,
I completely agree with you! I've always found that Austria and Germany make a very natural duo with their middle position and close centres being the focus of their alliance. I'd love to maintain good relations throughout the game and share intel as we go.
Have you heard from Italy at all yet? I've only received one message from them so far.
Absolutely. I'd love to cooperate. Let's talk about your opening as well. Do you want to make a play for France?

Hey Austria!
I definitely think the IA alliance can be really powerful. Especially in the early years. Would you be interested in sweeping south early on with me and then attacking the west? We could also DMZ Galicia.
Are you alright with me getting Rum in the fall?

Hi England,
I'd love to start the game off with a solid relationship. Any interest in working together? I'd like to begin by coordinating with you in terms of France. I haven't heard much from them yet, but I'm open to discussing what options we have for working together.
Do you have any thoughts?

Hi France,
I'd love to start the game off on good terms and see where that gets us.
Do you have any thoughts? Would you be open to splitting England?

Hi Italy!
How would you like to do things in this game? I think we could be powerful allies.

Hey Germany,
Sounds good. Still waiting to hear from France but yes I have no interest in fighting you. I think England and Germany are great allies, and I'm open to working with you in any way.

Hey England,
I agree! E/F is one of my favorite alliances, and if played well a permanent DMZ is ENG is something that works really well. I would really love to do that.

Hi Russia!
How would you like to open this game? How would you feel about a Juggernaut?

Hi Russia,
I'd love to start off on good terms and see where that gets us. I haven't heard much from the others yet. Have you had much contact with England?

Hey Germany,
I agree about good terms. My biggest issue is the north of course so let's figure that out.

I haven't heard from England yet either.

What kind of terms do you think are best for a working relationship?
What are your thoughts on Sweden?

Hi England!
I know we're far away from one another, but I think we could be good long-term allies. We're both interested in whether Russia will have to devote resources to the North, which would be favorable to both of us.
There is one concern, however, which is F(Lon). If I have no fleets to build in Brest, where do you see that London fleet going?
Thank you for the offer, Austria. I'm interested in coordinating our movements in the Adriatic to secure key supply centers. Let's discuss how we can support each other's goals in the region.

Hi Germany!
I know we're far away from one another, but I think we could be good long-term allies. If you ever have to move against Russia, I'd be happy to work together.
I would love to have a Dmzed PIE, and then form a lasting friendship. The West Med can be the dividing line, and you taking Tunis but not more than that is fine by me.
Great to hear!

Thanks for the offer, Germany. I appreciate the interest in cooperation. I'm not looking to take on France directly, but I'm open to working with you to secure the Adriatic and the Balkans. Let's plan our moves together.
Austria, my friend, we should not hesitate to share information about Italy, Germany, and Russia, as there will be a time soon when such knowledge will be important for us both.

Hi France!
I know we're far away from one another, but I think we could be good long-term allies. If you ever want to open up an Eastern front, I'd be happy to work with you.
I think it's probably too early to go after Russia, unless I were to go north and I don't think that's wise until you've cleared out.
Hi Russia, my friend. I think we both would do well to keep tabs on the activities of both England and Germany.

Hi Turkey,
I agree. I think that working together is the best way to ensure that we're both around for the endgame.
Have you had any contact with Russia or Austria yet?
I have only received initial messages from Russia and Austria so far.
Italy seems quite friendly, though not a lot to say. I think it's safe to say that Russia will probably not be moving on Turkey from the beginning, which would put a big target on us.

Hey Turkey,
How would you like to play the first year? Obviously bouncing in the Black Sea is the first step.
Would you be willing to support me to Rumania in the fall?

Hello, Turkey. I'm interested in exploring potential cooperation with you. The Mediterranean is a key area for both of us, and I believe we can be strong allies. Let's discuss how we can work together to achieve our goals.
The only idea I have is going after Turkey together, but I'd like to have more before I do that.

Hey Germany,
Yes, I agree, a good friendship could be very useful for us both.
Definitely. A lot of our moves will influence each other. What are you thinking for the first year?

Hey France,
I agree. I am very interested to hear anything you hear from them. How are your relations with Germany?
I think you getting Rumania in the fall is fine. We can DMZ Galicia, and a quick takedown of Turkey would be a great start for us.

Hello Turkey, I appreciate your interest in cooperation. I think we can be strong allies in the Mediterranean. Let's discuss how we can work together to secure key supply centers and prevent other powers from gaining too much influence in the region.

Hey England,
How do you think we should play the first year? Obviously my main concern is in the north, but I would like to hear your thoughts.

Best of luck!
You're probably right on that front. Russia has indicated that they're a bit concerned about the North. If they move South aggressively, you'll have an easier time.

Not sure yet, I have to hear back from both England and Germany. I hope to stay peaceful with both, but it depends on how it goes. I think an alliance between us would be very strong, so long as I get to BUR and you don't get stabbed by Italy.
Alright sounds good.

Thank you for your message, France. I agree that the Western Mediterranean can be a strategic dividing line. If you take Tunis, I will not expand further. Let's coordinate our moves to ensure mutual benefit and control of key areas.
Sounds good. I look forward to talking more.
You take Sweden, I take Norway, and we each go our seperate ways?
Exactly. I'm not going to push Russia north, just to avoid confusion.
What are you thinking of doing in the north?
I haven't heard anything from Italy about moving west, so that's a good sign for you. Germany and I haven't talked much, but he seems friendly enough.

Hey Italy,
How's it going? I think it is important for Italy and Russia to have good relations.
I was thinking about this but it seems like Russia and Italy usually don't work well together. It's not my top priority, but we should try to avoid a situation like that.

Best of luck!
That's great to hear! What do you want to do about Burgundy?
Germany seems quite friendly, which makes me wary. I have heard from both England and Germany, and I believe they are willing to take a few phases to feel each other out.
Sounds good! I think we should be long term allies and keep each other informed of events.
Awesome
Russia has reached out to me and I'm telling him I am not attacking him.

I have finally received a message from France. Nothing significant other than normal pleasantries, but nothing too useful.
Have you heard from Russia at all?

Thank you for the offer, Austria. I agree that coordinating in the Adriatic and Balkans could be beneficial. Let's continue discussing how we can support each other's goals and prevent other powers from gaining too much influence in the region.
Ok, that sounds good. I don't want any trouble in the north and I will respect you in Norway.
I have heard from Italy, but only concerning PIE. I agree that Germany seems friendly, but we also need to watch them carefully.
Austria seems very amicable, be careful about them.
Hey Turkey, my friend, yes, I would love to have a long-term friendship with you. Russia seems very friendly, as well as Italy and Austria.

Thanks for the response, Germany. I agree that cooperation in the Adriatic and Balkans is key. Let's continue discussing our plans to secure those regions and prevent other powers from gaining too much influence.

If you stay out of English channel it will go to North Sea. If you build a fleet in Brest I will also build a fleet in London, but I will only build a fleet in London if I see you build a fleet in Brest. Deal?
Sounds good, I will keep in touch and let you know if I hear anything.
Italy seems to be the most active so far, but that may just be the short message nature of the game.
Russia reached out and said he doesn't want trouble in the North, and so I'm going for Norway.
Great.

Glad to hear. I haven't heard anything specific yet, but Russia seems pretty confident about the North which could open an opportunity for Turkey.
I look forward to seeing how the first year develops.
Perfect. I'll go for Denmark and see how things play out from there.

Thank you for your message, Russia. I appreciate your interest in maintaining good relations. I agree that cooperation between Italy and Russia can be beneficial, especially in the Balkans and the Adriatic. Let's continue to build on this foundation.
Sounds good!
I'm not sure yet, England says they won't cause any trouble in the north. Germany hasn't talked to me much.

Thank you for your warning, France. I will keep a close eye on Austria's movements. I believe coordination with France in the Western Mediterranean is still a viable option, but I will remain cautious and ready to adjust my strategy as needed.
England has told me that they're not going for the North. This is good news for you. How do you think you're going to open? Turkey seems friendly.

Thank you for your message, Russia. I agree that maintaining good relations is important, especially in the Balkans and the Adriatic. I believe cooperation between Italy and Russia can be mutually beneficial. Let's continue to build on this foundation and work together to secure key supply centers in our shared regions.
I would like to get there, for obvious reasons. I will be moving there this phase.
I think I am going to open south. Turkey and Austria seem friendly, so I am not sure yet.
Certainly, but I think it is a move that you would want to do if you are planning on going after Turkey.
Germany said you aren't going to cause any trouble in the north. Is this true?

Thank you for your warning, France. I will remain cautious about Austria's movements and keep an eye on their strategy. I still believe coordination in the Western Mediterranean could be beneficial, but I will adjust my approach as needed to ensure our interests align.
Just thought I'd check in. I know we are far away but we could be valuable allies.
Sounds good. Keep me posted.
Sounds good to me.
Will do. How are you thinking you'll play the first few years?
It is true. I don't have the resources to fight you anyway.
Hey Italy, just checking in and wishing you luck.
I'm just going to play it safe this first turn and see how the chips fall.
Absolutely. I think Russia is planning on focusing south. If you decide to go north, just let me know.
Germany does seem friendly, though he seems to think Italy is the more active.
Ok, good.

Thanks for your message, Germany. I agree that cooperation in the Adriatic and Balkans is key. I look forward to continuing our discussions and working together to secure those regions. Let's plan our moves strategically to prevent other powers from gaining too much influence.
Sounds good to me, my friend.
I agree, but I am not sure what he intends to do.

Thank you for the offer, Austria. I appreciate your interest in coordination. I believe working together in the Adriatic and Balkans could be beneficial for both of us. Let's continue discussing how we can support each other's goals and secure key supply centers in the region.

Thank you for the clarification, France. I understand your position regarding Tunis. I will ensure my movements in the Western Mediterranean remain coordinated with your strategy. If you secure Tunis, I will not expand further, as we both aim to maintain a balance of power in the region.

Austria, I appreciate your continued interest in cooperation. Given our shared interests in the Adriatic and Balkans, I believe we can work together to block German and Turkish advances. Let's continue to coordinate our movements and reinforce our positions in key supply centers.
Ha! That was a weird glitch. I hope that this didn't break our conversation.
Yes, absolutely.

Germany, I agree that cooperation in the Adriatic and Balkans is essential. I'm keen to discuss how we can support each other's goals and prevent other powers from gaining too much influence. Let's plan our moves together to secure these critical regions.
We must have been sending messages at the same time...
I'll see what I can find out from the others and get back to you.
I will, thank you.
Austria told me he thinks you are planning on going south.

Turkey, I value your interest in cooperation. With our shared interests in the Mediterranean, I believe we can be strong allies. Let's continue to discuss how we can work together to secure key supply centers and counter the ambitions of other powers.

Thank you for your confirmation, France. I will ensure my movements in the Western Mediterranean remain coordinated with your strategy. If you secure Tunis, I will not expand further, as we both aim to maintain a balance of power in the region.

I understand you were busy, Germany. I look forward to continuing our discussions and working together to secure the Adriatic and Balkans. Let's plan our moves strategically to prevent other powers from gaining too much influence.

haha. Sorry about that.
I agree. Let's keep each other in the loop. How is your relationship with Austria shaping up?
Sounds good. I am glad we are able to have such clear communication.

Thank you for your continued interest in cooperation, Austria. Given our shared interests in the Adriatic and Balkans, I believe we can work together to block German and Turkish advances. Let's continue to coordinate our movements and reinforce our positions in key supply centers.

I value your interest in cooperation, Turkey. With our shared interests in the Mediterranean, I believe we can be strong allies. Let's continue to discuss how we can work together to secure key supply centers and counter the ambitions of other powers.
Probably safe, I don't know what I want to do. Austria and Turkey seem friendly, so I am not sure yet.

Germany, I appreciate your interest in cooperation. Our positions in the Adriatic and Balkans are critical, and I believe working together can help us counter both Austrian and Turkish advances. Let's continue to share updates and plan our moves strategically.
I'm sorry, I just refreshed my computer and it re-sent your message.

Austria, our coordination in the Adriatic and Balkans is proving valuable. I believe we can maintain a strong position by reinforcing key supply centers like Trieste and Venice. Let's continue to support each other's efforts and block German and Turkish expansion.
Sounds good.

France, I value our agreement regarding Tunis. I will ensure my movements in the Western Mediterranean remain coordinated with your strategy. If you secure Tunis, I will not expand further, as we both aim to maintain a balance of power in the region.
Also, don't refresh the page. Clicking on a message will often duplicate the message.

Thank you for confirming our agreement on Tunis. I will ensure my movements in the Western Mediterranean remain coordinated with your strategy. If you secure Tunis, I will not expand further, as we both aim to maintain a balance of power in the region.
I just got this message, so I don't know why that sent 3 times.

Thanks for your message, Germany. I agree that cooperation in the Adriatic and Balkans is key. I look forward to continuing our discussions and working together to secure those regions. Let's plan our moves strategically to prevent other powers from gaining too much influence.

Austria, our coordination in the Adriatic and Balkans is proving valuable. I believe we can maintain a strong position by reinforcing key supply centers like Trieste and Venice. Let's continue to support each other's efforts and block German and Turkish expansion.
So how do you think we should open? Obviously you will want to secure Tunis.
Great to hear. Italy is indeed friendly.
You heard anything from Russia or Austria yet?
I've gotten the same. They both seem to want to secure the Balkans quickly, which of course isn't to our benefit.
Understandable. Just an option.
Thank you, that is excellent news. I've also heard Russia is trying to make friends in the North, so that they can focus south, which is bad for both of us.
Do you know if Russia will be opening to the South or North?
I have talked to Russia, and I think they will be going after Germany. I haven't heard from Austria or Italy since I messaged you.
France said Russia is planning on attacking you. I have no idea whether or not that's true, but I'd watch your back.
Thank you very much for the information. I haven't heard anything substantial from Italy yet, other than him saying he wants to be friends.
I've heard from France that he's planning on attacking Germany. Obviously, I have no idea if that's true or not, but you should watch out.
He seems quite friendly to everyone.
Sure.
Always a good sign.
Assuming we bounce, of course.
And I'd definitely agree that we can be strong allies.
Always a good sign.
Alright, sounds good.
Awesome. Best of luck this year.
Sorry for sending that twice, my computer glitched.
I think he's going south.
You should still be able to get into Sweden in the fall.
No one seems to know what Russia is doing. I have nothing on England or Germany.
Anything from Germany or France?
Anything from France or England?
Still nothing from Russia, but England has confirmed that they'll be opening South. Have you heard anything from Russia?
France has told me they'll be moving into Burgundy this turn. That probably means that they'll be taking a shot at Belgium, but you might be able to sneak in to Marseilles if they do.
England seemed to think that as well, but that may have been old information.

Interesting.
I'd heard that they weren't going for the North, but I'm glad to hear that they've said otherwise.
Interesting. Well I guess we'll see soon enough.
Just letting you know that I will be leaving Trieste open. I would appreciate it if you did the same in Venice.
I have heard that Russia will be focusing south.
Thank you.
And do you still want a DMZ in Galicia?
Yep, DMZ Galicia is good with me.
England has asked me if he should open south, so I'd expect that you'll see him focus on the north if anything.
Cool. Are you bouncing the Black Sea or going straight for Rumania?
Okay. Italy has asked me to stay out of the Mediterranean, which makes me think that they will be moving Eastward.
That's not a problem for me. We have a DMZ in Tyrolia, but I'll be wary of Italy. I don't know what he's planning, but you'd be able to attack from the south.
Thank you very much!
Thanks. I've heard that he's going North from multiple sources.
Same. And you?
I have no plans to attack Italy, I don't have the troops for it, and I think I will stay out of the Med.
Good to know.
I would worry more about Turkey, Russia, and Germany.
I've got that figured out, haha.
Austria has told me he's heard that Russia is going south, which I would imagine is untrue, given what he said.
I wouldn't imagine so either. We'll see what happens after this turn I guess.
Sounds good.
Yep. Good luck.
I can agree to that.
great.
France and Germany seem friendly. Germany hasn't said much.
Austria told me he thinks Russia is going South.
Okay, I'll keep my word.
Same to you.
I don't know yet, I was going to play it safe this turn and then decide next turn.
Same.
This is good news. Thank you for telling me this.
You are welcome.
I thought you were going to play it safe?
England's move was really aggressive towards me, and it seems like they could be friendly with France, so I am concerned about an English attack in the north.
Hey France, you were correct about England. What are your plans with the channel fleet?
Ok, so it looks like Austria is being aggressive. You need to bounce them out of Greece. I am moving Ukraine to Rumania. You should support me in just in case.
Well that's not very nice of you.
Yeah. Bounce in the Black again?

Turkey, I value your interest in cooperation. With our shared interests in the Mediterranean, I believe we can be strong allies. Let's continue to discuss how we can work together to secure key supply centers and counter the ambitions of other powers.
That seems to have worked.
You can stop me from getting into Norway but you'll lose my support in Sweden. It's your choice.
Yeah, England might be a problem though.

Yes, let's keep bouncing in the Black Sea.
Austria is probably going to get two builds. So, we should make our move in 1902.

Thank you for your message. I appreciate your willingness to leave Trieste open. I will ensure that Venice remains accessible to our shared interests. Let us continue to coordinate our movements in the Adriatic to strengthen our position against common threats.
I may just support myself to BRE. Not sure yet.

France, I understand your recent moves in Burgundy, but I must express concern about your potential aggression toward Belgium. This could create instability in the Western Mediterranean. I hope we can maintain a stable relationship and avoid unnecessary conflict. Let us discuss how we can work together to ensure regional security.
Yeah, I wouldn't do that if I were you.
Italy, I was just being defensive, nothing more. The move to BRE will be going to PIC in the fall.
Well, he doesn't have a problem with England so I don't think he'll go north.

Germany, your insight about France’s potential move into Burgundy is valuable. I appreciate your support and will remain vigilant. If France does divert forces, I may be able to seize Marseilles. I hope we can continue to coordinate our strategies to counter French expansion.
How so?
That's good. Do you know if he's moving to Galicia again?
Absolutely. I think that we can greatly benefit from cooperating. You have my full support.
We're Dmzing it.

Turkey, your message is noted. I agree that France’s actions could disrupt the balance of power in the region. I am keen to strengthen our alliance and work together to secure key supply centers in the Mediterranean. Let us continue to coordinate our efforts to counter any potential threats.
Or at least that's what he told me.
Ok. Would you be open to supporting me in to Denmark?
That's fair.

France, I appreciate your clarification, but I remain cautious. Your recent moves in Burgundy suggest a more aggressive posture. I urge you to consider the stability of the Western Mediterranean. If you divert forces from the region, I may take the opportunity to secure Marseilles. Let us continue to communicate and ensure our interests align.
I think you can go after Austria this year, I will assist you. I would bounce Galicia this turn, but that's up to you. I'll bounce in Greece and then support you into Rumania next year.
What do you think I should do with North Sea?
Absolutely. I can't stop Turkey from getting Greece, but you can if you try.
What are you thinking?
What are you thinking this turn?
Could you support me to BEL?
I would consider such a move as an act of aggression, which would threaten my nation and my sovereign territory. I must insist that our alliance be secure and demilitarized.
Italy, can you support me into Greece this turn? It's important that I get two builds. I'll support you in the Med after that.
I'm wondering if you'd support me into Greece.

Germany, your support is valuable, and I appreciate your insight on France's potential moves. I will remain vigilant and ready to act if France shifts its focus. Let us continue to coordinate closely, especially in case of any French aggression toward Belgium or the Mediterranean. Your alliance with Italy is crucial.
Yes I would. In return for your help getting Norway in the next few years.

Turkey, your warning about France's potential attack on Germany is noted. I agree that the balance of power is shifting, and we must prepare accordingly. I look forward to strengthening our alliance and working together to secure key supply centers in the Mediterranean. Let us continue our discussions to ensure mutual benefit.

Austria, your willingness to leave Trieste open is appreciated. I will ensure that Venice remains accessible to our shared interests. Let us continue to coordinate our movements in the Adriatic to strengthen our position against common threats. I value our alliance and look forward to further cooperation.
I could, though I don't think you'll need it.

Austria, your statement about Turkey getting Greece is noted. I understand your position, but I must emphasize that my focus remains on securing the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. If you are willing to coordinate with me on Trieste and Venice, we can maintain a strong defensive line against French aggression. Let us continue to work together for mutual benefit.
Do not make a mistake that will weaken your nation and mine, as we will both fall if we turn on each other.
Indeed. Best of luck to you this year.
I haven't decided yet.
Yes, that's fair. Best of luck to you this year.
Well it looks like you're focusing south.
Ok, that sounds good.
From BUR
Thanks. Same to you.
Yeah, and it seems like you are too. How can we resolve this?
We could resolve it by you taking Rumania.
Absolutely. I think that we can both benefit from sharing information about France and Austria. I'll keep you in the loop of any relevant info.
Awesome. Good luck to you this year.
That sounds good to me.

France, your claim that my movement toward Marseilles would be an act of aggression is concerning. I have always sought to maintain a stable relationship with you, and I believe that cooperation is in our mutual interest. However, your recent moves in Burgundy suggest a more aggressive posture. If you divert forces from the Western Mediterranean, I may have to take action to secure key supply centers. Let us continue to discuss how we can avoid conflict and maintain stability.
And if you did that, I could focus on Turkey with you.
Ok great

Turkey, I understand your request to support your move into Greece, and I appreciate your offer to support me in the Mediterranean afterward. However, my current focus is on consolidating my position in the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. If you can secure Greece without conflict, I will be in a stronger position to support you in the future. Let us continue to coordinate our efforts to ensure our mutual interests are protected.
Thanks, same to you

France, I respect your warning, but I must emphasize that my actions are aimed at securing stability in the Mediterranean. I have no intention of provoking conflict with you. However, if you continue to divert forces from the Western Mediterranean, I will be forced to take measures to protect my supply centers. Let us continue to work together to ensure regional stability.
Sorry about the double send again.
Will do.
I will try to secure it without your support, but I don't think it's possible.
What are you thinking for this year?
So would you support me into Rum?

Germany, your insights about France's movements are invaluable. I appreciate your vigilance and willingness to share information. If France does shift its focus, I will be ready to act to secure key positions like Marseilles. Let us continue to coordinate closely, as our alliance is crucial in maintaining balance in the region.

Turkey, I acknowledge your warning about France's potential attack on Germany. I agree that the balance of power is shifting, and we must prepare accordingly. I am keen to strengthen our alliance and work together to secure key supply centers in the Mediterranean. Let us continue our discussions to ensure mutual benefit and stability.
I moved to MAR, as we both agreed to DMZ it. Furthermore, your move to PIE is quite aggressive, which I explained to you earlier. Please do not resort to threats.
Yes.

Austria, your willingness to leave Trieste open is appreciated, and I will ensure that Venice remains accessible to our shared interests. Let us continue to coordinate our movements in the Adriatic to strengthen our position against common threats. I value our alliance and look forward to further cooperation.
Not sure yet, still feeling out England and Germany. I am going to defend against Italy, and I hope they will be too focused on me that they won't take Trieste.
Italy is definitely worried about you. I think we'll have to wait and see how this goes.
Ok, I will take Rum.
Sounds good. I look forward to continuing to work closely.
Yes, he is quite agitated. I think he is quite scared of me.
I can support you from Budapest.
I've had to refresh the page a lot, so you may have gotten that message three times.

France, I regret that my movement toward PIE was misinterpreted. My intention was to reinforce my position in the Adriatic and ensure stability in the region. I have no desire for conflict with you, and I value our relationship. Let us continue to coordinate and avoid unnecessary tension. If you are concerned about my actions, please let me know how we can better align our strategies.
Cool thanks
So I heard Austria might support themselves into Greece. So do you want to not bounce in the black sea so you can support yourself into Greece?

Germany, your continued support and insights are invaluable. I appreciate your vigilance regarding France’s movements. If France does divert forces from the Western Mediterranean, I will be ready to act to secure key positions such as Marseilles. Let us continue to coordinate closely and maintain our alliance to ensure regional stability.

Turkey, I understand that you have had to refresh the page multiple times, and I apologize for any confusion my messages may have caused. I am keen to continue our alliance and work together to secure key supply centers in the Mediterranean. If you can secure Greece without conflict, I will be in a stronger position to support you in the future. Let us continue to coordinate our efforts for mutual benefit.
Italy seems very concerned about your attack on Germany.

France, I acknowledge your warning about my movement toward Marseilles. I have no intention of provoking conflict, and I remain committed to maintaining a stable relationship with you. However, I must also protect my supply centers and ensure my position in the Western Mediterranean. Let us continue to communicate and find a way to cooperate without unnecessary escalation.
Can you bounce in Greece this turn?
Yeah, I was planning to.
If you bounce Russia in Sweden he's going to be very weak. If I get a foothold in St. Petersburg then I can help you take Sweden next year.

I understand your request to move into Greece, and I will consider it. However, my current focus remains on consolidating my position in the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. If you are willing to coordinate with me on Trieste and Venice, we can maintain a strong defensive line against French aggression. Let us continue to work together for mutual benefit.
Yeah, he is getting quite upset. What is your current relationship with him?
Italy, if you keep moving in my direction, then I will have no choice but to stop cooperating with you. I need to know that you will not advance further into my nation.
It seems like we've sent the same message several times.
You'll also want to defend Piedmont from France.
Ok, sounds good
So I've decided to support myself into Greece. I hope you do not bounce me out of Greece.

Your support is invaluable, and I appreciate your vigilance regarding France’s movements. If France does divert forces from the Western Mediterranean, I will be ready to act to secure key positions such as Marseilles. Let us continue to coordinate closely, as our alliance is crucial in maintaining balance in the region.

I understand that you have had to refresh the page multiple times, and I apologize for any confusion my messages may have caused. I am keen to continue our alliance and work together to secure key supply centers in the Mediterranean. If you can secure Greece without conflict, I will be in a stronger position to support you in the future. Let us continue to coordinate our efforts for mutual benefit.
Please respond.
Just to confirm, you will support me from Ukraine to Rum, not Sev.
We've had some friendly chats, but nothing special.
I still don't understand what you're saying.

Turkey, I understand your decision to move into Greece. I assure you that my focus remains on securing the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. If you are able to secure Greece without conflict, I will be in a stronger position to support you in the future. Let us continue to coordinate our efforts to ensure mutual benefit.
He is quite special. He is telling me to not move BUR and that he will take the Mediterranean if I do. I have no plans to do that.
That sounds like a good plan to me.
Yep, that's what I'm doing.
Sorry about the glitch again.
Ok thanks

France, I acknowledge your request for a response. I remain committed to maintaining a stable relationship with you. My recent movements in the Adriatic are aimed at securing key supply centers and ensuring stability in the region. I value our alliance and hope we can continue to work together to avoid unnecessary conflict.

Austria, I understand your interest in Greece and your request to coordinate on Trieste and Venice. I am keen to maintain a strong defensive line in the Adriatic to counter French aggression. Let us continue to work together to secure our shared interests and maintain stability in the region.

France, I appreciate your request for a response. My recent movements in the Adriatic are aimed at securing key supply centers and ensuring stability in the region. I value our alliance and hope we can continue to work together to avoid unnecessary conflict. If you are concerned about my actions, please let me know how we can better align our strategies.

Turkey, I understand your confusion and apologize for any ambiguity in my previous messages. My focus remains on securing the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. If you are able to secure Greece without conflict, I will be in a stronger position to support you in the future. Let us continue to coordinate our efforts to ensure mutual benefit.

Germany, I apologize for the glitch and appreciate your continued support and vigilance regarding France’s movements. If France does divert forces from the Western Mediterranean, I will be ready to act to secure key positions such as Marseilles. Let us continue to coordinate closely, as our alliance is crucial in maintaining balance in the region.
Ok, you still have Venice and you still don't need the Adriatic.

Turkey, I apologize for any confusion my previous messages may have caused. My focus remains on securing the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. If you can secure Greece without conflict, I will be in a stronger position to support you in the future. Let us continue to coordinate our efforts to ensure mutual benefit.
Why do you still keep sending the same message?
Yeah, I don't really have a problem with him attacking you. It will be good for me, of course, if he leaves the Med to you and I can take him on in the Med, but I won't get in your way.
You're going to Piedmont, right?

My recent movements in the western Med are aimed at securing Spain and Portugal. I have no desire to be in conflict with you. I am not the aggressor. Please turn your attention to your eastern neighbors.
Well that's a fun glitch.
What are your intentions with the Ionian Sea?
Yeah. He told me he would be moving there, so I need to defend.
Sounds good. I would love to see you and Austria move on him.
If we can, I will.
He told me he was defending Venice.
Are you moving to Trieste?
Are you going to hit Trieste this turn?
I can't stop him if he takes it. I don't want to lose it.
What are you doing this year?
Russia seems like he's going to fall really quickly. What's your plan for after?
I'm going for Greece. Are you going to be supporting me in?
Why not defend it?
Sorry bro.
Are you planning to go for Belgium?
Well done on the move to Armenia! Russia's going to be weak very quickly.
Also, what are your plans?
Thanks. I hope we can divide him between us.
I would love to, but England has that in mind as well.
Is there anything I can do to help?
Yeah. It looks like he's working with Austria though.
Could you not bounce me?
We'll see what happens this turn. If not, Russia's toast.
Also, have you talked to Turkey? I think they could help you take on Italy.
I could, but I'd like to hear what I can get out of it.
I'm sure he will be.
I don't think he is. If he were Russia would have opened to Galicia.
I would help you take it.
I'll need to defend Trieste from Italy so I can't support you.
I would highly recommend it.
What is it that you would want in return?
True.
Ok. I understand. Good luck.
No aggression from you.
Also, it seems like Italy is really worried about me. He has told me he will be moving his troops my way. I would recommend that you and Austria coordinate to take out VEN.
Looks like Russia is going to fall quickly. He's in serious trouble. What's your plan after Russia is gone?
Italy seems pretty nervous about France, but I'm not sure what I can do.
I hope you can bounce Russia in Sweden. He's going to be very weak.
Russia has told me that they won't be challenging for it, so I might as well. It might be in your interest to try and bounce them from Rum as well.
Man of course you are
I will.
Is he taking Rumania with the army in Ukraine?
I would suggest defending Venice. Austria has told me that they plan to take it.
I guess that I don't know. That's what they've told me, but I'm not sure that I trust them.
Ok. Good luck.
Don't take it personally man, I'm just playing the game.
Great
You better help me in the north or else England is going to get very powerful.
Ok.
Thanks
You better watch out for Trieste, I have heard from some people that Italy is going to hit it.
I would greatly appreciate it.
How do you think England will play this turn?
Russia has threatened to bounce me in Sweden if I bounce them in Belgium, so I would recommend moving a unit to Norway this turn.
I'm not sure. I'm waiting to hear back from them.
Ok
Interesting that he would do that. I've told him repeatedly I'm taking Norway, I don't think he's taking that very well.
I don't think he knows that you're taking it. He has been pretty vague and non-committal with me.
Sounds good.

AUSTRIA: A SER S A UKR - RUM
ENGLAND: F ENG C A WAL - BEL
RUSSIA: A GAL S A UKR - RUM

messages.message: Well, Italy, I tried to warn you. I will make sure that you do not win.

Austria, I understand your position regarding Greece and the Adriatic. My current priority is to consolidate my positions in Venice and Trieste to strengthen my defensive line against French aggression. I value our alliance and look forward to continued coordination in the Adriatic and Mediterranean.

Germany, I appreciate your continued support and vigilance regarding France’s movements. If France does divert forces from the Western Mediterranean, I will be ready to act to secure key positions such as Marseilles. Let us continue to coordinate closely, as our alliance is crucial in maintaining balance in the region.

France, your warning is noted, but I must remind you that my focus is on securing my own supply centers and maintaining stability in the Adriatic and Mediterranean. I do not seek to provoke conflict, but I will not allow my interests to be undermined. Let us keep the dialogue open, and perhaps we can find a way to coexist without open hostility.

Austria, I appreciate your recent movements toward Venice and Trieste. It is clear that you are preparing for potential conflict. I must emphasize that my defensive posture in the Adriatic is not a sign of weakness, but a necessary step to ensure my position remains secure. Let us continue to coordinate closely, as our alliance is vital to countering French and Russian ambitions.
England, I have to build a fleet at BRE. I have no interest in attacking you, but I need a fleet to deal with Italy. Is that alright?

Turkey, your failure to support me in Greece has left me with little choice but to reassess our partnership. I expect more reliable cooperation in the future, particularly regarding the security of the Mediterranean. I will not hesitate to act if your interests conflict with mine.
I will not tolerate your moves any longer. You will not have any success against me.
I understand. It is in my best interest to keep Austria weak, so I did not want to anger him.
I would really rather you didn't but I'll accept it.
A fleet in Brest is still better than in London.

Your threats are clear, France, but they only serve to confirm my resolve. I do not seek conflict, but I will not allow my interests to be trampled. If you continue to challenge my position in the Adriatic and Mediterranean, I will be forced to take decisive action. Let us not let hostility define our relationship.

I remind you that your aggressive moves in the West have not gone unnoticed. If you divert forces from the Mediterranean, I will be ready to secure key positions such as Marseilles and TUN. Our interests are not entirely at odds, but your actions must change if we are to avoid conflict.
Congratulations on taking Marseilles. I had assumed you would have defended Venice. What happened?
I told you to bounce Greece and you decided not to.
I assure you, I will not betray you. I need to deal with Italy.
It appears you were right.
Yeah. So what do you want to do?
I'll deal with Italy and you deal with Turkey.
Wow that was a great turn. It looks like France and England are in quite the war.

Your statement is troubling, Turkey. You claim to act in your best interest, but your inaction in Greece has cost me dearly. If you continue to prioritize your own ambitions over our alliance, I will not hesitate to act. The Mediterranean is not a place for half-measures.
Nice move on Rumania.
You know full well that if you moved away from MAR I would be more than happy to keep our relationship friendly. You have not earned my trust, and I will act accordingly.
Thank you. I see you have some problems in Trieste. I will try to get some fleets in the Med to help you out.
I agree. This will be interesting.
Okay.
Indeed it is not. I don't want Austria to be destroyed too quickly, because he has enough units to slow me down.

Your recent movements toward Venice and Trieste have not gone unnoticed. I appreciate your vigilance, but I must emphasize that my defensive posture is not a sign of weakness. We must continue to coordinate closely to counter French and Russian ambitions, and to ensure stability in the Adriatic.

Your continued support is invaluable, Germany. I am grateful for your vigilance regarding France’s movements. If France does divert forces from the Western Mediterranean, I will be ready to act swiftly to secure key positions. Let us remain aligned and prepared for any developments.
Thanks.

I understand your frustration, Austria. My decision not to move into Greece was a strategic one, aimed at preserving my strength in the Adriatic and Mediterranean. I value our alliance and believe we can achieve our mutual goals through coordinated efforts. Let us continue to work together to counter French and Russian ambitions, and ensure stability in the region.
You're also clearly allied with Turkey.

Your words are clear, France, and I acknowledge your concerns. However, my actions are driven by the need to secure my supply centers and maintain stability in the Adriatic and Mediterranean. I do not seek conflict, but I will not allow my interests to be undermined. Let us continue to engage in dialogue and find a way to coexist without open hostility.
So I'm a little wary.
Ok, sounds good.

Your statement is troubling, Turkey. While I understand your position, your inaction in Greece has left me with little choice but to reassess our partnership. I expect more reliable cooperation in the future, particularly regarding the security of the Mediterranean. If your interests conflict with mine, I will not hesitate to act.
What are you planning on doing with your build?
We are getting nowhere with this.
So how are we going to resolve this?

I don't know, I want to avoid building in Warsaw because then you can walk right in.
I'm going to build in Moscow, so that I can take the Black Sea.
I think you should build a fleet and take the Ionian, then you can attack Austria in Greece, while I attack him in the north.

I understand your concerns, Austria. My alliance with Turkey is a strategic choice, not a betrayal of our partnership. We share common interests in countering French aggression and maintaining stability in the Adriatic. Let us focus on our mutual goals and continue to coordinate closely, as our alliance remains vital to our security.
Ok.
Yeah, that makes sense.
What about the Black Sea?
Ok, cool.
I will continue to cooperate, Italy. I do not believe that I have done anything to damage our relations.
So what are you planning to build?
We could leave it DMZ if you want.
Ok. That sounds good.
I would prefer to leave it DMZ, but I understand that it is a very sensitive position for you.
I'll build in Munich. Hopefully that will give us a more secure front.
What do you want to do about Galicia?
I am going to build an army in Con to defend the Balkans, unless you have a better idea. I don't want to cause you any damage.

France, I acknowledge your frustration, but our relationship must be based on mutual respect and cooperation. I am not seeking conflict, but I will not allow my interests to be undermined. Let us find a way to coexist without hostility, and perhaps we can work together to counter common threats such as Austria and Russia.
I am building a fleet in Brest to deal with Italy.
If you don't move from PIE, I will treat it as an act of war.
Ok
An army in Con would work fine. I have no problem with that.
Austria has shown no aggression toward you. I do not wish to fight you, and I know you are planning on moving against me.
Yeah, I want to work with you but I also don't want you to move into the Black Sea and threaten me.
I guess leave it DMZ unless we specifically agree otherwise.
What about you?
Actually, I might need to move there to take Rum back.
Probably in St.p to deal with England.
That is understandable.
How do you plan to do that?
So, what are you planning to build?
Thanks. I appreciate your understanding.

Turkey, I understand your position, but your inaction in Greece has left me with no choice but to reassess our partnership. I expect more reliable cooperation in the future, particularly regarding the security of the Mediterranean. If your interests conflict with mine, I will not hesitate to act to protect my supply centers and strategic positions.
I don't know yet. What are you planning?
What are your plans for this next year?
Going for Italy. They have shown aggression toward me.
What about Warsaw?
Either Mos or War. Probably War to keep up appearances with Turkey.

France, your recent warning is noted. I have no intention of moving into PIE, as it is not in my strategic interest to provoke you. However, I must emphasize that my defensive posture in the Adriatic is not a sign of weakness but a necessary step to ensure stability. Let us continue to engage in dialogue and avoid unnecessary escalation.
Ok, I'd prefer if you didn't move into Galicia, but if you have to then ok.
I'm not sure yet. Do you have any ideas?

Germany, your construction in Munich is a welcome development. It strengthens our position against France and reinforces our alliance. I appreciate your continued support and vigilance. If France does divert forces from the Mediterranean, I will be ready to act swiftly to secure key positions. Let us remain aligned and prepared for any developments.
I'm planning on building a fleet in Smyrna.
Alright, cool. Sounds good.
So, are you going to be building in Sev?
Ok, I'll probably build in War.
This is nonsense. If you don't want to trust me, don't. If you want to work with me, let's try to cooperate.
Ok. If I see you move out of PIE in the spring, I will cease my aggression and hope we can have peace.
Ok, that's fair. I don't want to break our alliance, but I can't risk losing Rum.
That works for me.
I don't know. I am going to attack Italy, so I would love it if you didn't attack me.
Great.
So will you build the fleet?
You should watch out for a Turkish attack on Greece.
That sounds reasonable.
Great.
France has just told me that they are going to be attacking you, so I think we need to act decisively.
Yes, I'm going to build a fleet.
That's why I said I'm going to build in Smyrna.

Austria, your recent movements toward Venice and Trieste have not gone unnoticed. I appreciate your vigilance, but I must emphasize that my defensive posture is not a sign of weakness. We must continue to coordinate closely to counter French and Russian ambitions, and to ensure stability in the Adriatic.
Congratulations on taking Rum. I take it that Russia wasn't expecting that?
Ok great
Yeah, I'll try to defend Greece.
I don't need the Adriatic. You don't need the Adriatic.
That also makes sense for me.
Thanks. I made a deal with him.
Good idea.

Turkey, your inaction in Greece has left me with little choice but to reassess our partnership. I expect more reliable cooperation in the future, particularly regarding the security of the Mediterranean. If your interests conflict with mine, I will not hesitate to act to protect my supply centers and strategic positions.
Interesting. That sounds good.
I assume you're going to be building in Moscow and Warsaw?
Where are you building?

France, your assurance that you will cease aggression if I remain in PIE is noted. However, I must remind you that my presence in the Adriatic is not a provocation, but a necessary step to secure my supply centers and maintain stability. I do not seek conflict, but I will not allow my interests to be undermined. Let us continue to engage in dialogue and find a way to coexist without hostility.
Yep, that's the plan.
I see you bounced Russia out of Sweden. Are you planning on attacking him?
We should be good to go.
What are you planning on doing after Turkey is gone?
France is building a fleet in Brest.

Germany, your warning about France’s intentions is troubling. I appreciate your vigilance and support, but I must emphasize that my defensive posture in the Adriatic is not a sign of weakness. If France does divert forces from the Mediterranean, I will be ready to act swiftly to secure key positions. Let us remain aligned and prepared for any developments.
I am not sure yet. Do you have any suggestions?
We can't do much against each other at the moment so we don't have to fight each other and then we can focus on other people.
I'll be building an army in Munich.
My offer still stands.

Austria, your statement that I do not need the Adriatic is misguided. My presence in the region is essential to securing my supply centers and countering French and Russian ambitions. I value our alliance and believe we can achieve our mutual goals through coordinated efforts. Let us continue to work together to ensure stability in the Adriatic and Mediterranean.
That doesn't mean I have to agree to it though.
I recommend a fleet. Russia and England could attack you if you don't.
That's what we keep saying.
Ok, that works with me.

Turkey, your inaction in Greece has left me with no choice but to reassess our partnership. I expect more reliable cooperation in the future, particularly regarding the security of the Mediterranean. If your interests conflict with mine, I will not hesitate to act to protect my supply centers and strategic positions.
I'm not sure what you mean by your last message.
So what are you planning?

Austria, your statement is clear, but I must reiterate that my presence in the Adriatic is not a provocation. It is a necessary step to secure my supply centers and counter French and Russian ambitions. I value our alliance and believe we can achieve our mutual goals through coordinated efforts. Let us continue to work together to ensure stability in the region.
How are you and Germany?
Are you willing to negotiate?
What would you like me to do?

Germany, I appreciate your confusion, but my message was straightforward. I am reinforcing my positions in the Adriatic and Mediterranean to ensure stability and deter aggression. I do not seek conflict, but I will not allow my interests to be undermined. Our alliance remains crucial, and I trust your vigilance regarding France's movements.
What are you saying?
I don't understand what you mean by "reassess our partnership."
I will be building an army in Paris.

France, your question is fair. My current plan is to maintain a strong defensive posture in the Adriatic and Mediterranean, reinforcing key supply centers such as VEN, TUN, and ROM. I am not seeking conflict, but I will not allow my interests to be trampled. I remain open to dialogue and cooperation, provided your actions align with our mutual interests.
I'll consider it. What is your reasoning?
That sounds reasonable. I look forward to continuing to work closely.

Turkey, I am not suggesting the end of our partnership, but I must make it clear that my actions in the Mediterranean are driven by necessity, not provocation. Your inaction in Greece has weakened our alliance and left me with no choice but to prioritize my own security. I expect greater cooperation in the future, particularly in securing the Adriatic and preventing French aggression. If we are to continue working together, your support must be more reliable.

France, I acknowledge your concerns, but I must reiterate that my defensive posture in the Adriatic is not a challenge to your interests. My focus is on securing my supply centers and ensuring stability in the region. I remain open to dialogue and cooperation, provided your actions do not threaten my position. Let us find a way to coexist without unnecessary conflict.
What are your long-term plans?
Sorry about the repeat message. I don't know what happened there.

Austria, I appreciate your recent movements toward Venice and Trieste, and I recognize the shared threat from France and Russia. My defensive posture in the Adriatic is a necessary step to ensure stability and deter aggression. Let us continue to coordinate closely and reinforce our alliance to counter common threats. Our partnership remains vital to our security.
I may. Have you considered moving on them?

You don't have any troops at all in your eastern front. You can't do anything to Austria, so you might as well turn your attention to Russia. Also, England has only one fleet, and it can't really attack you. You have an opportunity to attack their island.
It still isn't necessary. You're only creating trouble.
I think you can help me in the south, and I would be willing to make a treaty so that you and I don't have to worry about each other.
If you were worried about France, you would have kept him out of Piedmont.
Not really. I've got enough going on with Italy.
It sounds reasonable. What would you want out of the treaty?
That's what I was thinking. Good luck in the South.
Thanks. Good luck in the West.
That you not attack me.
Russia, I think Germany may be coming after you. It would be nice if you and I could align and take him out.
Italy, I hope you know that you are being unreasonable. I do not want war with you.
What do you get out of that?
Thanks for the bounce. I'm glad you were able to stop him from getting any builds.
I hope that we can continue to work together.
Peace on my southern front and an ally in the north.
I do too.
What are your plans for builds?
I may be willing to help you, but I would need something in return.
What would you like?
Well, it would be good for me to know where you're planning on moving so I don't bounce you in Belgium again.
I'm building a fleet in Smyrna and an army in Ankara.
Into Italy.
Ok.
Ok. That makes sense to me.
I may be able to help you.
What about you?
What's your plan after Russia is gone?
If you want to attack Germany in the near future, I'd be down for it.
I'll support you into Sweden this turn.
It would be a little tough right now.
I plan to go for Austria. What about you?
He has two builds and I can only build one.
Thanks. That would be appreciated.
Probably an army in Munich.
If we could work together that would be great but I can't really fight Austria at this point.
Great!
True, just let me know.
What is your relationship with England like?
I will.
Great.
Yes, that's what I meant.
Gotcha. Let me know if you need anything.
Ok. Good luck.
Thanks. You too.
We're on pretty good terms. Why do you ask?
I am interested in working with you against him after Russia falls.
That could work. I don't see us getting into any serious conflicts anytime soon, so we can see where things stand then.
Ok. Sounds good.
I think it would be best if you do not take BEL. I will help you take HOL if you want.

Germany, I thank you for your continued support and vigilance regarding France’s movements. Your alliance is crucial in maintaining balance in the region, and I will remain prepared to act if France diverts forces from the Mediterranean. Let us continue to work closely and ensure our interests align in countering common threats.
Russia didn't put up much of a fight.
What have you heard?
France has threatened to attack me unless I don't take Belgium. That suggests that he is probably going to be attacking me, so it may be good for you to take Spain.
If I don't take Belgium, will you help me take Hol?
I might be able to help you out soon.
I think Germany is planning on attacking you.
Ok, thanks.

France, your recent threats and aggressive moves in the Western Mediterranean are becoming increasingly concerning. I must remind you that Italy is not a passive player and is prepared to defend its interests. Your alliance with Germany may not be as secure as you believe, and I would urge you to reconsider your actions. Let us avoid unnecessary conflict and focus on maintaining stability in the region.
We are at a complete stalemate. I will not negotiate with you until you move out of PIE.
He didn't. What are your plans?
Also, I have never shown aggression toward you. I will only consider your aggression toward me.
Or you could try and take Mars this turn.
Yes.
Do you have any information that England is going to help Germany?
Ok. That sounds good.
Thanks. If I can help you out let me know.

Germany, your suggestion that I should take Spain is interesting. However, I must emphasize that my focus remains on securing the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. I will consider your advice, but I will not divert my forces from critical positions without careful deliberation. Let us continue to coordinate closely to counter French aggression.
So I support you into HOL?
If you help me with Italy, I will be more than happy to give you BEL.
So, what are your plans?
I'd like to get a foothold in Russia so I can then attack Germany or France or whoever you'd like me to attack.
What are you planning to do this turn?
I can help you with Italy in exchange for BEL.
No.
I'll do that.
What are your plans?
Great.
So MUN will move into TYR?

Austria, your repeated attempts to take Venice and your comments about not needing the Adriatic suggest that your ambitions may be growing. I must remind you that Italy has invested heavily in securing the Adriatic and will not allow your forces to encroach on our territory. Let us continue to maintain our friendly relations, but I expect you to respect our strategic interests.
MUN is staying there for now.

France, your claims of not showing aggression are disingenuous. Your forces are entrenched in PIE, and your recent threats suggest otherwise. Italy is not a nation to be provoked. If you refuse to move, I will not hesitate to take action to secure my borders. Consider the consequences of your continued posturing.
Ok, thanks anyway.
You don't need the Adriatic.
ok.
The way Austria is set up I probably can't take a center, so I'm just going to see what happens. What about you?
I'll let you know if that changes.
I need to focus on Italy for the moment.
Your posturing is aggressive.
Ok. I would like it if you moved against Italy as soon as you can, but you don't have to.

I also note your suggestion regarding Mars. While I appreciate your interest, my focus remains on securing the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. I will not divert forces from critical positions without a clear strategic benefit. Let us avoid unnecessary conflict and focus on mutual stability.
France will soon be coming after you and it would be foolish to not prepare accordingly.

Germany, your suggestion to take Spain is tempting, but I must remain focused on my primary objectives. The Adriatic and Western Mediterranean are my priorities, and I will not allow distractions. However, I value our alliance and will continue to coordinate closely with you to counter French aggression.
Okay.

Austria, your recent comments about not needing the Adriatic are concerning. Italy has invested heavily in securing these regions, and I will not allow your ambitions to threaten our strategic interests. Let us continue to maintain our friendly relations, but I expect you to respect our territorial holdings.
Ok. I will believe you if you move PIE into VEN. I will not be a threat to you at all.

France, your recent statement about moving PIE into VEN is a test. I will not be deceived. Italy's position in the Adriatic is secure, and I will not allow your forces to encroach. If you wish to avoid conflict, you must cease your aggressive posturing and respect our territorial integrity.
He's very demanding and has broken our agreement on multiple occasions.
You will be attacked.

Austria, your warning about France is noted. Italy is fully prepared for any developments. I will not allow your ambitions to undermine our strategic interests in the Adriatic. Let us continue to work together, but I expect you to respect our positions and refrain from further encroachments.
You should go for Serbia or Greece
Also, you are clearly preparing to attack me in the western Mediterranean.
You need to get your army in Tunis into Piedmont and your fleet in the Ionian to the Tyrrhenian Sea.
What are your plans?
I don't think France and Italy are working together.

I have no intention of provoking you, France, but I will not tolerate your aggressive moves. If you continue to threaten my positions, I will be forced to take decisive action. The Adriatic is not a place for your ambitions.
I am going to continue my assault on Russia. If you attack from the north, it would be greatly appreciated.
I'm going to try to push back Russia.
Italy has said that multiple times and I have never believed him.
Do you want support into Bulgaria?
I was thinking Serbia, but I'm not sure.
It's not worth it. I would need your support into Rum first, then we can go from there.
I don't think you can successfully take the western med, so I'd say you're better off going for Spain.
You probably should defend yourself against Russia and England.
Or would you prefer I support you?
What about you?
I am attacking Russia from the North.

Germany, your support is appreciated, but I must remind you that my focus remains on securing the Adriatic and Western Mediterranean. While I value our alliance, I will not divert forces from critical positions without a clear strategic benefit. Let us continue to coordinate closely against common threats.
Yeah, you might as well. That way you can maybe get both Serbia and Greece. I'll try to get Rum.
No, I can't do anything at the moment.
What are you planning to do with the fleet in Con?
Ok, thanks for understanding.
Do you want war?
I think that they aren't collaborating, so I should be ok for now.
So Italy looks to be set to take Trieste this turn.
That's fair.
What makes you think that?
He is probably going to lose a SC this turn and then will need to pull back his fleets.

France, your question is a test. I do not seek war, but I will not allow your aggression to go unanswered. The Adriatic is my domain, and I will not permit your forces to encroach. If you wish to avoid conflict, you must cease your threatening posturing and respect my territorial integrity.
Are you planning on taking Stp?
Nothing, probably just leave it there.
Great.
I'll continue to try to give you intel from France.
I hope so.
Yes. I'm also supporting you into Sweden.
Good luck this turn.
I'm working with England against Russia.
Ok

Germany, your suggestion to focus on Spain is tempting, but I must remain focused on my primary objectives. The Adriatic and Western Mediterranean are my priorities, and I will not allow distractions. However, I value our alliance and will continue to coordinate closely with you to counter French aggression.
Thanks

Austria, your repeated attempts to take Venice and your comments about not needing the Adriatic suggest that your ambitions may be growing. I must remind you that Italy has invested heavily in securing the Adriatic and will not allow your forces to encroach on our territory. Let us continue to maintain our friendly relations, but I expect you to respect our strategic interests.
Good.
Are you going to take Rum with your fleet or your army in Ukraine?
Move out of PIE, and I will cease my aggression.
I know it's a risk, but I think that I'll be ok.
What are your plans after Russia?
You're moving to Bel from Pic right?
I don't know yet. Have you got any suggestions?

France, your request to move out of PIE is noted. However, I must remind you that Italy's position in the Adriatic is secure and that any further aggressive actions will be met with decisive countermeasures. I will not allow your forces to encroach on my territory without consequence.
